channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipping outside 52 alexandra drive, 20th May
item.description:
More junk dumped outside no. 52

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 58, Alexandra Drive, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken gate, 20th May
item.description:
Gate no longer closes shut. Compromises safety of children and dogs who may run out into the busy road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Robsart Street, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black sack and rubbish dumped on grass verge in Merrilees, between Corbylands Road and woodbine Road, 20th May
item.description:
Only spotted today (20/05/26). Broken glass around tree, black sack and supermarket bag with contents dumped in grass verge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 61, Corbylands Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking a drop curb repeatedly, 20th May
item.description:
This car repeatedly keeps blocking a drop curb which is needed for access to multiple flats as well as multiple businesses meaning that no vehicles can pass (including e bikes which are needed for deliveries for the companies).

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Frogley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Only one blue cycle lane creates collision, 20th May
item.description:
On Ontario street, there is only one blue cycle lane drawn. This creates bike collision! Westbound cyclists (going towards the traffic light) have a guiding blue cycle lane from Keyworth St to Ontario St. This guiding blue cycle lane disappears, so cyclists move on the only one they see, which is for cyclists going eastbound. This creates dangerous collisions between cyclists! I have seen two crash in front of me and I myself fell because of the sudden stop. You should continue the westbound guiding blue cycle lane up to the traffic light to guide cyclists accordingly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ontario Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown vegetation, 20th May
item.description:
Overgrown vegetation on pavement - needs to be cut down to avoid being an obstruction.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car blocking entrance to cul de sac and blocking residents from getting in out of there private property, 20th May
item.description:
Around half 5 two young lads was seen leaving this car, There was another car rightfully parked next to now has moved to allow people to squeeze through risking scratching there car. It’s blocking the entrance to woodfall drive cul de sac. Number plates are visually loose the vin number has a card over it so you cannot see it i suspect it’s stole needs to be moved asap as it’s restricting access. Just left in the middle of the road a black nissan NA20 DTX

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodfall Drive, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown vegetation on pavement, 20th May
item.description:
Overgrown vegetation on pavement - needs to be cut down to avoid being an obstruction.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Heavy lorries are regularly parking on the pavement, 20th May
item.description:
Heavy lorries are regularly parking on the pavement outside my street in order to unload goods. This is causing serious disturbance and safety concerns for residents. The repeated presence of large commercial vehicles on the pavement is resulting in: * vibration and shaking of the residential building, * obstruction of pedestrian access, * potential structural concerns, * excessive noise and disturbance, Despite repeated issues, no effective enforcement or preventative measures appear to have been implemented. I request that the Council urgently: * investigate the use of the pavement by heavy goods vehicles, * assess possible damage and vibration impact on nearby residential buildings, * enforce loading restrictions, * consider protective measures such as barriers, CCTV enforcement, or loading restrictions, * confirm what action will be taken and within what timeframe.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Plough Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Streetlight outside 24 Chalcot Crescent, 20th May
item.description:
You kindly attended a while back to reattach the lantern bulb guard that had fallen off - it had been placed here previously to stop light streaming in through the bedroom windows at night - but it has been positioned very badly and currently makes very little difference to the glare. We are still unable to sleep as the light remains incredibly bright. The guard needs to be re-positioned and, bearing in mind that we live on the bend and are consequently exposed to more of the bulb, perhaps the guard needs to be made longer so that the cover is adequate? Also, aesthetically speaking, whoever attended time used a bright red tape to attach the guard - which looks absolutely awful and completely spoils the beauty of the lantern. Do you not have plain old black tape! Thank you so much - looking forward to sleeping through the night!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Chalcot Crescent, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bushes blocking footpath, 20th May
item.description:
Saw a lady with a pram have to walk towards oncoming traffic because they couldn't get past the overgrowth

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Crofton Avenue, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water leakage on A5109, 20th May
item.description:
Odourless water spilling/pooling/leaking on A5109 near the pavement between 12 and 26 Totteridge Lane ever since the road was resurfaced here earlier this year..
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Totteridge Lane, Whetstone, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big black box things, 20th May

Big black box things that have been on adjacent property a long time are now on the footpath.

item.title: Voi bike parked illegally, 20th May
item.description:
How is it acceptable for e bike companies to have their bikes parked in bays which are for residents entering or leaving the car park or for emergency vehicles, but it is not acceptable for me to park my car there for 24 hours?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Road, Hoxton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog turd bandit, 20th May
item.description:
This guy lets his dogs shit down the street all the time. Today I was unlucky enough to have to watch the live action foulness be released in front of me in the pathway. I asked him if he thought it was okay to let his dogs shit along the path and then I asked him if he had any intention of cleaning it up. He didn't so I took the photo. The video of his dog fouling wouldn't upload. It was clear as day he has never cleared up his dogs shit, because why should he...

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hervey Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pot hole, 20th May
item.description:
Been getting worse over last couple of months. Whole road needs doing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bexley Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wooden pallets, 20th May
item.description:
This looks like building marerials

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Crooms Hill, Greenwich

item.title: Nails in step on slide, 20th May
item.description:
3 nails sticking out of penultimate step on staircase to the slide in north playground Bottom step has already rotten away Staircase needs maintaining to make it safe for children

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Martello Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
